lesson: Give a base Exception subclass a .code attribute and derive specific error types from it, so callers can catch the whole family via the base class.
task: custom exception hierarchy with error codes

lesson: Remember try/else runs only when the try block raised nothing, and finally always runs afterward even when except or else returns a value.
Wrap a single try/except/finally around one yield in a @contextlib.contextmanager generator to get commit-on-success, rollback-on-exception, and unconditional cleanup in one reusable context manager.

Use `raise NewError(...) from original` to set __cause__ and __suppress_context__=True explicitly; omitting 'from' still links the error via __context__ but leaves __cause__ None and __suppress_context__ False.
task: exception chaining semantics: explicit vs implicit

lesson: Use ExitStack.enter_context in a loop for a runtime-sized set of context managers; on mid-loop failure it unwinds only entered resources in reverse order, and callbacks also run LIFO.
task: ExitStack for a dynamic list of resources with partial-failure unwind

lesson: To swallow one specific exception type with cleanup attached, make __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) return True only for that type; for no-cleanup suppression, use contextlib.suppress(ExcType) instead.
task: selective exception suppression via __exit__ vs contextlib.suppress

lesson: except* returns matched leaves still wrapped in the original nested ExceptionGroup shape, not a flat tuple; recursively flatten by walking .exceptions before treating results as plain exceptions.
task: except* exception-group handling with nested groups (PEP 654)

lesson: Never let a @contextmanager generator catch an exception and yield again in the same except -- gen.throw() forbids it, raising RuntimeError('didn't stop after throw()'); retry outside the with.
task: generator-based context manager retry pitfall and exception translation
